mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the effective federal funds rate reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows and stimulate credit-sensitive corporate investment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value 4.09% as of Oct 2025
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals accelerating labor market slack that precedes consumption pullbacks and negative corporate earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 5-year rolling window, or the mean return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and increases in the unemployment rate represent a broad-based demand contraction that compresses corporate revenues and profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised down to 731700 and UNRATE at 4.3% as of Aug 2025
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-UNRATE interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC magnitude above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of top-quintile interaction readings.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent combined with month-over-month CPI inflation below 0.2 percent causes equity markets to reprice forward earnings multiples on expectations of imminent monetary easing, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase below 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.09% and CPIAUCSL vintage 324.368 as of Sep 2025
failure_condition: During triggered periods, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) compared to non-triggered periods, or the hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and factory orders that translate into near-term earnings surprises, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down to 101.6247 as of Aug 2025
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive month-over-month INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,300 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand that precedes broader equity market drawdowns, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1291.0 as of Aug 2025
failure_condition: Triggered instances (HOUST < 1300) produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the unemployment rate is above 4.0 percent, because job losses in a slack labor market trigger larger consumption discounting than during tight labor markets.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change negative and UNRATE > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 158472 and UNRATE at 4.3% as of Aug 2025
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 4.0%, negative PAYEMS months fail to produce significantly negative 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05 vs. positive PAYEMS months), or the differential return is less than -1%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5 percent combined with negative three-month momentum in industrial production generates a policy-mistake risk premium that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and INDPRO 3-month percent change negative
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.09% and INDPRO revised down as of Aug 2025
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-INDPRO interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC magnitude above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of top-quintile joint stress readings.
